compositional_truth_check.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss (No viable specimen identified in black pot).
compositional_truth_check.P3_Pothos: Present (Black Pot, upper left, with white rabbit anchor).
compositional_truth_check.P4_Silver_Guest: Present (Black Pot, lower center, solitary leaf).
compositional_truth_check.New_Interventions: Eggshell fragments observed in P4 soil.

vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry is largely accurate regarding P1 and P3. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent; the black pot designated for it appears to contain only soil and debris. P4 (Silver Guest) is present as a single, isolated leaf structure.

plant_audit.P1: Stable, low-density foliage. No significant growth or decline observed.
plant_audit.P2: Systemic loss. No plant matter visible.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf remains turgid. Rabbit anchor is stable.
plant_audit.P4: Single leaf remains upright but shows no signs of new development or secondary leaf emergence.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, appears moist/damp across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Eggshell fragments present in P4. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic matter or spills.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Minimal postural shift in P4; P3 remains unchanged.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No growth or decline detected. The biome is in a state of stasis.

anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P4 soil (likely a calcium amendment or substrate additive).
P2 pot is devoid of the expected Mexican Mint.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical desk environment is currently in a state of vegetative stasis. P3 (Pothos) and P1 (String of Nickels) maintain their baseline health. P4 (Silver Guest) persists as a single-leaf specimen with no signs of active growth. The most critical observation is the total absence of P2 (Mexican Mint), which must be classified as a systemic loss. The presence of eggshells in P4 suggests an intentional, albeit uncatalogued, soil intervention.
